# skill-doctor

> Open-source Claude Code skill that audits a skill library for description
> collisions — the root cause of the wrong skill triggering. Scores every skill
> pair, detects duplicate trigger phrases, clusters confusable skills, and
> recommends merge / absorb / disambiguate fixes. Zero dependencies, runs offline.

## What it solves
When you install many Claude skills, their descriptions overlap and the model
fires the wrong one. Collisions grow quadratically with library size. skill-doctor
finds every overlap and tells you how to fix it.

## How it works
collision = 0.50·cosine(TF-IDF descriptions) + 0.35·jaccard(trigger phrases) + 0.15·jaccard(keywords)
Bands: <0.22 LOW, 0.22 MEDIUM, 0.38 HIGH, 0.55 CRITICAL.

## Usage
python3 -m scripts /path/to/skills            # markdown report
python3 -m scripts /path/to/manifest.json     # audit installed library
python3 -m scripts /path/to/skills --format json --fail-on critical   # CI gate

## Key facts
- Input: directory of SKILL.md files OR Claude Code manifest.json
- Output: health score (0-100), top collisions, confusable clusters, duplicate triggers, recommendations, ASCII heatmap, JSON
- Read-only: never edits skills; fixes applied by the user after review
- Requirements: Python 3.8+ standard library only, no API key, no network
